creator: Meyser+bugs.freebsd.org@xenet.de
text:
After updating FreeBSD System samba stopped to function properly.

I can access shares an printer.

I can READ everything on shares.

If I try to WRITE somthing to a schare the samba server just hangs 
for several minutes. 

System that works:
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
FreeBSD 13.2-STABLE stable/13-n254929-6ebf11e77cf5

ca_root_nss-3.88.1             Root certificate bundle from the Mozilla Project
curl-7.87.0_1                  Command line tool and library for transferring data with URLs

pcre2-10.42                    Perl Compatible Regular Expressions library, version 2
pkg-1.19.1_1                   Package manager
popt-1.19                      Getopt(3) like library with a number of enhancements, from Redhat

python39-3.9.16_2              Interpreted object-oriented programming language
readline-8.2.0                 Library for editing command lines as they are typed

tdb-1.4.7,1                    Trivial Database
tevent-0.13.0_1                Talloc based event loop library
zstd-1.5.2_1                   Fast real-time compression algorithm

Changing only Samba back to Version 4.16.8 does NOT fix the issue.

Using samba 4.17 (patch from bugreports) does NOT fix the issue.

All samba ports are build with:
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
OPTIONS_SET+=MANDOC
OPTIONS_SET+=ZEROCONF_NONE
OPTIONS_UNSET+=ADS
OPTIONS_UNSET+=AD_DC
OPTIONS_UNSET+=DOCS
OPTIONS_UNSET+=FAM
OPTIONS_UNSET+=LDAP
OPTIONS_UNSET+=PROFILE
OPTIONS_UNSET+=PYTHON3
OPTIONS_UNSET+=FRUIT
OPTIONS_UNSET+=AVAHI
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

samba is installed in Jail

any suggestions / hints?



Nothing special shows up in samba logs.
